description_long: This object is a historical artillery cannon mounted on a wooden carriage with four solid wheels. The cannon barrel is long, cylindrical, and made of dark, weathered metal, featuring ornate details near the breech and muzzle. The carriage is constructed from heavy wooden beams, reinforced with metal brackets and bolts, and includes rope rigging for maneuvering. The wheels are simple, circular, and appear to be made of solid wood, attached to the carriage with metal axles. The overall appearance suggests a robust, utilitarian design typical of cannons used in historical urban fortifications or naval settings. The surface of the cannon is rough and rusted, while the wood shows signs of age and wear.
description: A weathered metal cannon mounted on a sturdy wooden carriage with four solid wheels and rope rigging, showing signs of age and use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the cannon's muzzle, the symmetrical arrangement of the wheels, and the reinforced wooden carriage.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full length of the cannon barrel, ornate details near the breech, and the structure of the wooden carriage and wheels.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the breech of the cannon, the back of the carriage, and the arrangement of the wheels and rigging.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the length of the barrel, carriage details, and wheel placement.
attribute_weapon_mounts: One fixed cannon barrel mounted centrally on the wooden carriage.
attribute_camouflage_pattern: None visible; surfaces are solid, weathered metal and wood.
